checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: Revenues value=1752330631.05 (2012-07-01 to 2013-06-30); AccountsReceivableNetCurrent value=236017924.29 (2013-06-30), a +40.2% AR increase vs +25.9% revenue increase
Revenues value=2715866349.37 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30); AccountsReceivableNetCurrent value=323454493.45 (2015-06-30), AR growth (+11.3%) trailed revenue growth (+24.8%)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill value=520406991.87 (2010-06-30) growing to Goodwill value=1147636790.69 (2015-06-30), a 2.2x increase vs revenue growing 3.0x over the same period

IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill value=200144646.37 (2010-06-30) growing to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill value=658109789.61 (2014-06-30), roughly a 3.3x increase, comparable to revenue growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit value=251288715.64 on Revenues value=899061400.81 (2009-07-01 to 2010-06-30), ~27.9% gross margin
GrossProfit value=624910440.69 on Revenues value=2715866349.37 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30), ~23.0% gross margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent value=1590012.94 (2010-06-30); AccountsReceivableNetCurrent value=115376955.34 (2010-06-30), allowance ~1.38% of gross AR
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent value=905115.37 (2015-06-30); AccountsReceivableNetCurrent value=323454493.45 (2015-06-30), allowance ~0.28% of gross AR
evidence.location: FY2015 comparative data in FY2016 Q3 10-Q

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filing_date=2016-03-15; CORRESP filing_date=2016-03-24; UPLOAD filing_date=2016-04-08
NT 10-K filing_date=2007-08-29; NT 10-Q filing_date=2007-11-09; 10-K/A filing_date=2008-03-26; 10-K/A filing_date=2008-10-28

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data points span from fiscal 2008-2009 (e.g., NetIncomeLoss period 2008-07-01 to 2009-06-30) through 2016-03-31 (e.g., AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2016-03-31), covering roughly 8 fiscal years with both annual and quarterly granularity

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction/under-provisioning of the allowance for doubtful accounts relative to a rapidly growing gross receivables balance, which understates bad debt expense and overstates both net receivables (asset) and net income.

Potential acceleration of revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms (e.g., channel stuffing) in FY2013, evidenced by receivables growing far faster than revenue in that single year, possibly inflating reported sales relative to underlying demand.

Purchase-accounting judgments in a serial-acquisition program (rapid goodwill/intangible build-up) may mask underlying organic margin erosion, understate acquired liabilities/integration costs, or defer expense recognition, contributing to the observed gross margin compression despite strong top-line growth.

rationale_evidence.quote: GrossProfit value=251288715.64 on Revenues value=899061400.81 (FY2010, ~27.9% margin)
GrossProfit value=624910440.69 on Revenues value=2715866349.37 (FY2015, ~23.0% margin)
Goodwill value=520406991.87 (2010-06-30) growing to Goodwill value=1147636790.69 (2015-06-30)

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables fell from ~1.4% (2010) to ~0.28% (2015-2016) despite AR nearly tripling, indicating potential under-reserving that could overstate net income and receivables.

Receivables grew markedly faster than revenue in FY2013 (+40.2% vs +25.9%), a one-year anomaly that could reflect aggressive revenue recognition or credit-term extension, though plausibly acquisition-related.
Gross margin compressed from ~28% to ~23% over FY2010-FY2015 amid heavy M&A-driven goodwill/intangible growth, raising questions about acquisition accounting and true organic profitability.
Active SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence in March-April 2016, shortly before the cutoff date, suggests the filer was under SEC review at that time.
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income on an annual basis, and inventory turns were stable/improving, which are positive earnings-quality counter-signals reducing overall concern.
